topics: Introduction to analysis of deformable bodies; types of external loads – normal, shear, bending, and bearing stress; linear and shear strains; Hooke's law; stress-strain diagrams
Concepts of isotropy, orthotropy, and anisotropy; Young's modulus, bulk modulus, and rigidity modulus; Poisson's ratio; relationship between elastic constants
Deformation in axially loaded bars – uniform cross section, varying cross section, dissimilar materials; principle of superposition; thermal effects in simple and composite bars
Torsion of shafts; torsion theory of elastic circular bars – assumptions and limitations; polar modulus; torsional rigidity; shaft design for torsional load

Beams – classification; diagrammatic conventions for supports and loading; differential equations between load, shear force, and bending moment; shear force and bending moment diagrams of cantilever and simply supported beams with point load/UDL; point of inflection
Pure bending; flexure formula for beams – assumptions and limitations; section modulus; flexural rigidity
Shearing stress in beams – shearing stress formula; shear stress distribution in beam section; derivation and problems for rectangular section only; assumptions and limitations
Deflection of beams; moment-curvature relation – assumptions and limitations; double integration method; Macaulay's method
Stress on an inclined plane due to uniaxial stress, biaxial stress, and two normal stresses accompanied by shear stresses; principal planes and stresses; Mohr's circle of stress
Buckling and stability of long columns; Euler's buckling/crippling load for columns with different end conditions – Euler equation derivation for both ends hinged only; Rankine's formula
